title: Disaster Risk Science

prerequisites.text: Acceptance for Honours or Master's specialising in EGS.

outline: Through a transdisciplinary lens, this course explores broad concepts, legislative and policy frameworks, practical applications, tools and skills influencing disaster risk science. The course begins by exploring different scholarly arguments about the unnatural state of natural disasters, then investigates theoretical models and case studies that underscore the fundamentals of disaster impact minimisation. It interrogates various ways in which humans, through environmental and socio-economic factors, drive vulnerability and subsequently create disaster risk.
dp_requirements: At least 80% attendance and submission of all assignments.
